name: Kintsugi Tax
skip_reason: The provided website https://kintsugi.ai does not correspond to Kintsugi Tax, the sales tax automation product. It resolves to an unrelated technology company page covering aerospace, robotics, power, and defense projects (e.g. 'Team Kinetiz', 'Eneron Magnus', 'AutoGo'). The actual Kintsugi Tax product operates at a different domain (trykintsugi.com), which was not the URL supplied for this research task, so no verifiable facts about Kintsugi Tax could be sourced from kintsugi.ai.

enrichment.short_description: Lago is an open-source billing platform for usage metering, invoicing, entitlements, and complex subscription pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lago is an open-source billing platform offering usage metering, invoicing, and revenue analytics for scaling any pricing model.

features.title: Usage metering
features.description: Real-time usage ingestion and metering to support flexible, consumption-based pricing models.

features.title: Billing and invoicing
features.description: Automated invoice generation, credit notes, and support for progressive billing as an add-on.

features.title: Entitlements and plan management
features.description: Configure plans, entitlements, prepaid credits, coupons, and custom contracts for customers.

features.title: Payment and accounting integrations
features.description: Connects to payment providers (Stripe, Adyen, GoCardless), accounting systems (NetSuite, Xero), and tax software (Avalara, Anrok).

features.title: Revenue analytics
features.description: Dashboards for tracking billing and revenue performance across customers and plans.

features.title: Dunning and alerting
features.description: Automatic dunning workflows and real-time alerts for usage and payment events.

features.title: Lago AI
features.description: AI-powered billing intelligence built into the platform.

features.title: Self-hosted or cloud deployment
features.description: Deployable via the open-source self-hosted edition or through Lago Cloud, avoiding vendor lock-in.

pros: Open-source with a public GitHub repository (10,223+ stars), allowing self-hosting and code inspection
SOC 2 Type II certified with regular third-party penetration testing
Handles high scale, reportedly processing 1M events per second and $829M in invoices monthly (as of October 2025)
Broad integration ecosystem spanning payments, accounting, tax, CRM, data, and automation tools
Supports hybrid pricing that combines subscriptions and usage-based billing
cons: No public pricing for Lago Premium/Cloud — requires contacting sales or booking a demo
Some features, such as progressive billing, tax integrations, dunning, and 24/7 support, are offered as paid add-ons
Free usage is effectively limited to the self-hosted open-source edition, which requires infrastructure to run

faqs.question: Is Lago open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lago's code is open source and publicly available on GitHub, with over 10,223 stars, and can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does Lago have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Lago can be run for free via self-hosted open-source deployment. The Lago Premium/Cloud offering is quote-based, with pricing available on request.

faqs.question: What integrations does Lago support?
faqs.answer: Lago integrates with payment providers like Stripe, Adyen, and GoCardless, accounting tools like NetSuite and Xero, tax software like Avalara and Anrok, CRMs like HubSpot and Salesforce, data platforms like S3 and ClickHouse, and automation tools like Zapier and n8n.

faqs.question: Is Lago secure and compliant?
faqs.answer: Lago is SOC 2 Type II certified, undergoes regular third-party penetration tests, and supports SSO (including Okta), RBAC, and audit logs.

faqs.question: Can Lago be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lago offers both self-hosted deployment (on-premises or in your own cloud) and a managed Lago Cloud option.

use_cases.title: Usage-based billing for AI and API companies
use_cases.description: Meter real-time usage and convert it into flexible, consumption-based invoices.

use_cases.title: Compliant billing for fintechs and banks
use_cases.description: Manage complex billing structures with the compliance controls needed in regulated financial services.

use_cases.title: Telecom and IoT usage billing
use_cases.description: Handle high-volume, real-time usage metering and invoicing for connected devices and telecom services.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted billing infrastructure
use_cases.description: Deploy an open-source billing system in-house to avoid vendor lock-in and retain full data control.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams that want to own their billing infrastructure

features: Self-hosted deployment on your own infrastructure
Access to Lago's open-source codebase on GitHub
Core billing and usage metering functionality
Billable metrics and usage ingestion with real-time alerts
Entitlements, plan configuration, customer portals, prepaid credits
Invoice generation, credit notes, and progressive billing (add-on)
Payment integrations, automatic dunning, analytics dashboard, AI assistants
API key permissions, RBAC, and SSO via Okta

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lago?
paragraphs: Lago is an open-source billing platform that gives teams transparency, control, and flexibility to manage and scale pricing models. It provides infrastructure for usage metering, invoicing, entitlements, and revenue analytics.

The platform supports hybrid pricing that combines subscriptions with usage-based billing, along with coupons, prepaid credits, and custom contracts. Lago can be deployed as a self-hosted open-source instance or through the managed Lago Cloud, and it is SOC 2 Type II certified.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lago is for
paragraphs: Lago is built for engineering and finance teams at AI companies, enterprises, fintechs, banks, and IoT/telecom providers that need complex, compliant billing without relying on a third-party black box or building billing infrastructure from scratch.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, real-time usage metering and billing platform for usage-based pricing, now operating as part of Kong, Inc.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenMeter: open-source, real-time usage metering and billing for usage-based pricing, now part of Kong, Inc.

features.title: Real-time metering
features.description: Processes events at high throughput (millions of events per second) to track customer usage as it happens.

features.title: Usage limits and gating
features.description: Enforces usage limits and quotas with low-latency checks on edge infrastructure.

features.title: Flexible product catalog
features.description: Supports plans, credits, usage-based pricing, and commitments for structuring billable products.

features.title: Revenue insights
features.description: Surfaces expansion opportunities and identifies fast-growing customers from usage data.

features.title: CRM integration
features.description: Pushes revenue and usage signals into CRM platforms so sales teams can act on them.

features.title: Self-service billing changes
features.description: Lets teams deploy pricing changes in minutes without a full engineering cycle.

features.title: Open-source self-hosting
features.description: Available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license for teams that want to self-host instead of using the managed cloud.

features.title: SOC2-compliant cloud
features.description: Cloud deployments are SOC2-compliant with role-based access control (RBAC) and single sign-on (SSO).

pros: Open-source under Apache 2.0, so it can be self-hosted at no license cost
Built for high-throughput, real-time event processing (millions of events per second)
SOC2-compliant cloud offering with RBAC and SSO
Integrates with common data infrastructure: Kubernetes, Amazon S3, OpenTelemetry, Stripe, and CRM platforms
Used in production by companies such as Trigger.dev, Beam, Requestly, and Traceloop
cons: Detailed cloud pricing tiers are not published; the pricing page directs visitors to contact sales
As of September 2025, OpenMeter was acquired by Kong, Inc., and its pricing page now routes signups through Kong Metering & Billing, which may signal future changes to the standalone product
No publicly stated founding year, headquarters, or team size

faqs.question: Is OpenMeter open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenMeter is available on GitHub under the Apache 2.0 license for self-hosted deployments.

faqs.question: Does OpenMeter offer a free tier?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenMeter Cloud offers a free tier for getting started, alongside paid plans available by contacting sales.

faqs.question: Who owns OpenMeter?
faqs.answer: OpenMeter joined Kong, Inc. in September 2025. OpenMeter Cloud continues to operate for existing and new customers under this new ownership.

faqs.question: What SDKs or languages does OpenMeter support?
faqs.answer: OpenMeter provides SDKs/clients for TypeScript/Node.js, Python, and Go, plus a cURL-accessible REST API.

faqs.question: Is OpenMeter secure and compliant?
faqs.answer: OpenMeter's cloud deployments are described as SOC2-compliant and support RBAC and SSO.

use_cases.title: Metering AI and API usage for billing
use_cases.description: Tracks per-event or per-token usage from APIs and AI products in real time to feed usage-based billing.
use_cases.audience: SaaS and AI product teams charging based on usage

use_cases.title: Enforcing usage limits and quotas
use_cases.description: Applies low-latency usage gating so products can cut off or throttle access once a customer's quota is reached.
use_cases.audience: Product and engineering teams needing real-time quota enforcement

use_cases.title: Surfacing revenue expansion opportunities
use_cases.description: Feeds usage trends into CRM systems to flag fast-growing accounts to sales and revenue operations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenMeter?
paragraphs: OpenMeter is an open-source usage metering and billing platform that turns events, logs, and metrics into billable revenue. It processes usage data in real time — at a scale of millions of events per second — so teams can track consumption, enforce usage limits, and power usage-based pricing.

The product is available both as a managed OpenMeter Cloud offering (SOC2-compliant, with RBAC and SSO) and as a self-hosted, open-source deployment under the Apache 2.0 license. In September 2025, OpenMeter joined Kong, Inc., though OpenMeter Cloud continues to serve existing and new customers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenMeter is for
paragraphs: OpenMeter is aimed at developers, product teams, and revenue operations staff at SaaS, API, and AI companies that need to meter usage and bill for it — including teams that want to self-host via its open-source SDKs (TypeScript/Node.js, Python, Go) rather than rely solely on a managed cloud.

enrichment.short_description: Amberflo is a usage metering and AI monetization platform with an AI Gateway for 1,500+ LLM models and usage-based billing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Amberflo: usage metering and AI cost monetization platform with an AI Gateway and usage-based billing. Plans start at $99/month.

features.title: Real-time usage metering
features.description: Tracks usage of APIs, tokens, and events across services in real time.

features.title: AI cost tracking and governance
features.description: Provides operational controls such as Cost Guards and Budgets to manage and cap AI spend.

features.title: AI Gateway
features.description: Offers unified access to more than 1,500 LLM models with built-in retries and fallbacks.

features.title: Intelligent Model Router
features.description: Routes requests across models for cost optimization and prompt optimization.

features.title: Credits system
features.description: Supports prepaid credit drawdowns with automated tracking against usage.

features.title: Flexible billing models
features.description: Supports usage-based, outcome-based, and subscription pricing structures.

features.title: Revenue recognition automation
features.description: Automates revenue recognition workflows to support compliance and audit readiness.

features.title: Per-unit cost and margin tracking
features.description: Calculates per-unit cost and margin per customer for profitability analysis.

pros: Dedicated AI Gateway supports 1,500+ LLM models with built-in retries and fallbacks
Publishes concrete entry-level pricing ($99/month Startups plan) rather than quote-only pricing
30-day free trial available to evaluate the gateway and baseline FinOps visibility
Supports multiple billing models (usage-based, outcome-based, subscription) in one platform
Enterprise tier includes SSO and role-based access control
cons: Entry-level Startups plan caps at $10,000 billing volume and 10k meter events per month before overage applies
Growth plan pricing rises quickly with request volume, from $599/month up to $1,499/month depending on tier
Enterprise pricing is quote-only and requires contacting sales
No publicly stated founding year, headquarters, or team size

faqs.question: Does Amberflo offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial is available to evaluate the AI Gateway and baseline FinOps visibility.

faqs.question: How much does Amberflo cost?
faqs.answer: The Startups plan starts at $99/month (up to $10,000 billing volume and 10k meter events/month). The Growth plan ranges from $599 to $1,499/month depending on request volume, and Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: How many LLM models does Amberflo's AI Gateway support?
faqs.answer: The AI Gateway provides unified access to more than 1,500 LLM models with built-in retries and fallbacks.

faqs.question: What billing models does Amberflo support?
faqs.answer: Amberflo supports usage-based, outcome-based, and subscription pricing, plus a prepaid credits system.

faqs.question: Does Amberflo's Enterprise plan include security features like SSO?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise/Custom plan includes single sign-on (SSO) and role-based access control.

use_cases.title: Controlling AI/LLM spend
use_cases.description: Uses Cost Guards and Budgets to monitor and cap variable AI and LLM costs in real time.
use_cases.audience: AI-native companies managing variable LLM costs

use_cases.title: Automating usage-based billing
use_cases.description: Meters usage and automates invoicing for usage-based, outcome-based, or subscription pricing.
use_cases.audience: SaaS and infrastructure platforms billing on consumption

use_cases.title: Routing across multiple LLM providers
use_cases.description: Uses the AI Gateway and Model Router to access 1,500+ models with retries, fallbacks, and cost/prompt optimization.
use_cases.audience: Engineering teams building products on multiple LLM providers

features: Up to $10,000 billing volume per month
Up to 10k meter events per month

pricing_plans.price: From $599/month (tiered up to $1,499/month by request volume)
features: Up to $100,000 billing volume per month
Up to 500k meter events per month

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Amberflo?
paragraphs: Amberflo is a usage metering and AI monetization platform that unifies metering, cost attribution, and billing operations. It tracks usage of APIs, tokens, and events in real time, and pairs that with an AI Gateway offering access to more than 1,500 LLM models with built-in retries and fallbacks.

Beyond metering, Amberflo provides AI cost governance tools (Cost Guards, Budgets), a prepaid credits system, and support for usage-based, outcome-based, and subscription billing models, along with revenue recognition automation and per-customer margin tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Amberflo is for
paragraphs: Amberflo is built for AI-native companies, infrastructure providers, and SaaS platforms that need to track and control variable AI/LLM costs while billing customers on usage-based, outcome-based, or hybrid pricing models.

enrichment.short_description: Usage-based billing infrastructure that processes usage data, rates bills, and automates quote-to-cash for B2B SaaS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: m3ter is usage-based billing infrastructure for B2B SaaS, automating rating, invoicing, and quote-to-cash. Now part of Salesforce.

features.title: Usage data ingestion
features.description: Ingests and processes raw usage data at scale to feed downstream billing calculations.

features.title: Advanced rating engine
features.description: Performs bill calculations with flexible pricing logic to support complex usage-based pricing models.

features.title: Revenue leakage prevention
features.description: Aims to reduce under-billing, which m3ter states can cost companies 4-7% of revenue.

features.title: Billing automation
features.description: Automates quote-to-cash workflows to reduce manual billing operations.

features.title: Real-time usage dashboards
features.description: Provides reporting and dashboards for monitoring usage and billing in real time.

features.title: CRM/ERP integration
features.description: Connects with existing systems such as Salesforce, NetSuite, and Looker rather than replacing them.

pros: Purpose-built for complex, usage-based B2B SaaS pricing models
Integrates with existing CRM/ERP systems (Salesforce, NetSuite, Looker) instead of requiring a rip-and-replace
Founded by team with prior usage-based pricing experience from GameSparks (acquired by Amazon)
Backed by Salesforce following its acquisition of m3ter
cons: No public pricing; requires a demo or sales conversation to get a quote
No free trial or free plan advertised on the site
Now owned by Salesforce, which may affect long-term product roadmap and independence
Designed primarily for mid-size and large B2B SaaS companies, less suited to very small teams

faqs.question: Is m3ter free to use?
faqs.answer: No public free plan or free trial is listed. Pricing is based on a custom quote after scheduling a demo.

faqs.question: Who owns m3ter?
faqs.answer: m3ter is now part of Salesforce, following a definitive acquisition agreement announced by the company.

faqs.question: Does m3ter replace my CRM or ERP?
faqs.answer: No. m3ter is built to work alongside existing systems such as Salesforce, NetSuite, and Looker rather than replace them.

faqs.question: Who is m3ter built for?
faqs.answer: m3ter targets mid-size and large B2B SaaS companies that need to operationalize usage-based pricing strategies.

use_cases.title: Usage-based billing operations
use_cases.description: Finance and billing operations teams use m3ter to process usage data and calculate accurate, complex bills.

use_cases.title: Launching new pricing models
use_cases.description: Product and engineering leaders use m3ter to implement and test new usage-based pricing strategies without building billing infrastructure in-house.

use_cases.title: Preventing revenue leakage
use_cases.description: Companies use m3ter's rating engine to reduce under-billing errors that m3ter states can cost 4-7% of revenue.
use_cases.audience: Revenue and finance teams at B2B SaaS companies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is m3ter?
paragraphs: m3ter is usage-based billing infrastructure that processes usage data, performs advanced rating calculations, and automates quote-to-cash operations for B2B SaaS companies. It is designed to sit alongside existing CRM and ERP systems, such as Salesforce, NetSuite, and Looker, to handle the complexity that legacy systems struggle with when pricing is usage-based.

m3ter was founded in 2020 by John Griffin and Griffin Parry, who previously built GameSparks (acquired by Amazon in 2017) and brought their usage-based pricing experience into m3ter. The company is now part of Salesforce, following a definitive acquisition agreement.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who m3ter is for
paragraphs: m3ter is aimed at mid-size and large B2B SaaS companies with usage-based or hybrid pricing models. It is used by finance and billing operations teams to automate rating and invoicing, by product and engineering teams to launch new pricing strategies, and by sales and customer success organizations that need accurate, real-time usage and billing data.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native revenue automation platform for billing, revenue recognition, collections, and usage metering.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zenskar automates billing, revenue recognition, collections, and usage metering for finance teams with complex pricing models.

features.title: Billing automation
features.description: Automates invoicing for subscription, usage-based, hybrid, and value-based pricing models.

features.title: Revenue recognition
features.description: Generates audit-ready revenue recognition compliant with ASC 606 and IFRS 15 without manual spreadsheets.

features.title: Collections and dunning
features.description: Manages receivables and automated dunning to help collect payments faster.

features.title: Usage metering
features.description: Sends, aggregates, and monitors usage data to feed metered and hybrid billing.

features.title: Contracts Agent
features.description: AI-powered agent that processes contracts to automate downstream billing terms and generate invoices.

features.title: Entitlements management
features.description: Automates and manages customer credits and entitlements without requiring developer involvement.

features.title: Customer portal
features.description: Low-code customer portal that customers can use to view invoices and billing details.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Tracks SaaS metrics and revenue insights, with natural-language querying of billing data.

pros: All plans include the same feature set; the company states it does not gatekeep features by plan
Over 100 integrations, including HubSpot, Stripe, Avalara, and NetSuite
AI Contracts Agent automates invoice generation from contract terms
SOC 2, ISO 27001, and GDPR compliant
Offers a free pilot program and sandbox environment for evaluation before purchase
cons: No public pricing; all three plans (Starter, Standard, Enterprise) require a custom quote
No self-serve free plan; access requires going through a sales-driven pilot process
Founding year and company size are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Zenskar have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Zenskar does not offer a public free plan, but it provides a free pilot program of flexible duration and a sandbox environment for testing.

faqs.question: What are Zenskar's pricing tiers?
faqs.answer: Zenskar offers three tiers — Starter, Standard, and Enterprise — all with custom, quote-based pricing rather than public rates.

faqs.question: Do all Zenskar plans include the same features?
faqs.answer: Zenskar states it does not gatekeep features by plan; core modules like billing, revenue recognition, collections, and usage metering are available on all plans, with differences mainly in support level and SLAs.

faqs.question: Where is Zenskar based?
faqs.answer: Zenskar's headquarters is listed at 169 Madison Avenue #2201, New York, NY 10016.

faqs.question: Does Zenskar use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zenskar includes an AI-powered Contracts Agent for automating invoice generation from contract terms, an AI agents marketplace, and natural-language querying of billing data.

use_cases.title: Automated invoicing for complex pricing
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Zenskar to automate invoicing across subscription, usage-based, and hybrid pricing models without manual workarounds.

use_cases.title: Audit-ready revenue recognition
use_cases.description: Controllers use Zenskar's RevRec module to produce ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliant revenue recognition without spreadsheets.

use_cases.title: Usage-based product monetization
use_cases.description: Engineering and product leaders use Zenskar's usage metering and entitlements to launch and manage usage-based or credit-based pricing.

use_cases.title: Faster collections
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Zenskar's dunning and collections tools to reduce days sales outstanding.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zenskar?
paragraphs: Zenskar is an AI-native revenue automation platform that covers the order-to-cash cycle, including billing, revenue recognition, collections, usage metering, and analytics. It is designed to replace manual billing workarounds and spreadsheets for companies with complex pricing models such as subscriptions, usage-based, and value-based billing.

The company is headquartered in New York and has raised a $15M Series A. Zenskar reports customer outcomes including faster month-end close and reduced time spent on invoicing, and connects to more than 100 external systems, including HubSpot, Stripe, Avalara, and NetSuite.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zenskar is for
paragraphs: Zenskar serves finance teams, controllers, and founders at SaaS, cloud infrastructure, AI, fintech, e-learning, dev tools, CPaaS, and retail tech companies that need to automate billing and revenue recognition for complex or usage-based pricing without relying on spreadsheets.

name: SaaSOptics
skip_reason: www.saasoptics.com now permanently redirects (301) to maxio.com/saasoptics, whose headline states 'SaaSOptics is now Maxio.' SaaSOptics no longer exists as a standalone product/brand — it was rebranded following the Maxio/Chargify merger — so this entry is a name mismatch and cannot be enriched as SaaSOptics.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native revenue automation platform for B2B billing, collections, and ASC 606 revenue recognition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tabs automates B2B billing, collections, and ASC 606 revenue recognition with AI included in every plan.

features.title: Automated billing
features.description: Generates invoices for subscription, usage-based, and hybrid billing models without custom logic.

features.title: Collections
features.description: Automates payment collection and cash application to accelerate cash flow.

features.title: Revenue recognition
features.description: ASC 606-compliant revenue recognition automation for finance teams.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Real-time revenue analytics and custom reporting options.

features.title: Contract management
features.description: Manages contract terms so billing stays accurate against what was agreed.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Purpose-built AI automation for billing workflows and collections, included in every plan at no extra cost.

features.title: Accounting integrations
features.description: Connects with QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Oracle, Rillet, and HubSpot, plus a developer API.

pros: AI capabilities are included in every plan at no additional cost
Implementation is positioned as fast ("live in weeks, not quarters")
Handles complex hybrid and usage-based billing models without custom logic
SOC 2 compliant with audit-ready controls
Integrates with major accounting platforms (QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Oracle, Rillet)

faqs.question: What does Tabs do?
faqs.answer: Tabs is an AI-native revenue automation platform that manages billing, invoicing, collections, and ASC 606 revenue recognition for B2B companies.

faqs.question: How much does Tabs cost?
faqs.answer: The Launch plan starts at $2,000/month for companies with up to $5M in annual revenue and up to 100 active contracts. Growth, Scale, and Enterprise plans are custom-priced based on revenue and contract volume.

faqs.question: Does Tabs offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial or free plan is listed on the Tabs pricing page.

faqs.question: Does Tabs include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tabs states that AI is included in every plan at no additional cost, used for billing workflow and collections automation.

faqs.question: What accounting tools does Tabs integrate with?
faqs.answer: Tabs integrates with QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Oracle, Rillet, and HubSpot, among 14 total listed integrations, plus a developer-friendly API.

use_cases.title: Subscription and usage-based billing
use_cases.description: Automates invoice generation for companies running subscription, usage-based, or hybrid billing models.

use_cases.title: ASC 606 revenue recognition
use_cases.description: Automates revenue recognition to stay compliant with ASC 606 while reducing manual close work.

use_cases.title: Collections and cash application
use_cases.description: Automates collections to accelerate cash flow and reduce billing errors at scale.

use_cases.title: Multi-system accounting integration
use_cases.description: Connects billing data with existing accounting stacks like QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, and Sage Intacct.

features: Custom workflows and multi-entity support available

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tabs?
paragraphs: Tabs is an AI-native revenue automation platform built for B2B companies that need to manage the full billing and revenue lifecycle, from contracts and invoicing to collections and revenue recognition.

The platform automates invoice generation across subscription, usage-based, and hybrid billing models, and includes ASC 606-compliant revenue recognition, real-time reporting, and AI-driven automation for billing workflows and collections, included in every plan at no extra cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tabs is for
paragraphs: Tabs is aimed at B2B finance and RevOps teams handling complex or high-volume billing, ranging from companies with up to $5M in annual revenue on its Launch plan to enterprises with $50M+ in revenue and unlimited contracts on its top tier.

It connects with existing accounting stacks such as QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Oracle, and Rillet, making it suited to teams that need billing automation layered on top of their current finance systems rather than a full accounting system replacement.

enrichment.short_description: Sequence is a CPQ, billing, and revenue recognition platform built for B2B finance teams to automate revenue operations.
enrichment.headquarters: London, UK and New York, US
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sequence combines CPQ, billing, and revenue recognition for B2B finance teams, with published plans starting at $799/month.

features.title: Native invoicing
features.description: Automated invoicing built into the platform for recurring and custom B2B deals.

features.title: Usage metering
features.description: Tracks usage to support consumption-based and usage billing models.

features.title: Advanced pricing models
features.description: Supports bespoke contract terms and custom pricing scenarios for complex B2B deals.

features.title: Revenue recognition
features.description: Available as an add-on on Core and Scale plans for automated revenue recognition.

features.title: ERP/CRM integrations
features.description: Connects with systems such as NetSuite and Salesforce to sync billing and revenue data.

features.title: Sales tax integrations
features.description: Included on the Core and Scale plans to handle tax calculation on invoices.

features.title: Custom reporting dashboard
features.description: Optional add-on across all plans for tailored revenue reporting.

features.title: Compliance certifications
features.description: Platform supports ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR compliance across all tiers.

pros: Publishes a fixed starting price ($799/month) for its entry-level Growth plan, unlike many competitors that are fully quote-only
Combines CPQ, billing, usage metering, and revenue recognition in one platform
Backed by well-known investors including Andreessen Horowitz and Salesforce Ventures
Supports ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR compliance across all plans
Higher tiers add dedicated onboarding, migration services, and custom integrations for scaling companies
cons: Core and Scale tiers require bespoke, quote-based pricing rather than published rates
No free trial or free plan is mentioned on the pricing page
Revenue recognition is an optional add-on rather than included by default on the Growth plan
Specific AI capabilities behind the "AI Revenue platform" positioning are not detailed on the site

faqs.question: Does Sequence offer a free trial or free plan?
faqs.answer: No free trial or free plan is listed on the Sequence pricing page; the site directs visitors to book a demo.

faqs.question: How much does Sequence cost?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan is published at $799/month for startups with under $1M in annual revenue. The Core and Scale plans use bespoke pricing based on billed revenue and required features.

faqs.question: Where is Sequence based?
faqs.answer: Sequence describes itself as built in London and New York.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does Sequence support?
faqs.answer: Sequence states support for ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR across all plans.

faqs.question: Can I add revenue recognition to any plan?
faqs.answer: Revenue recognition is available as an optional add-on on the Core and Scale plans.

use_cases.title: Early-stage billing for startups
use_cases.description: The Growth plan targets startups under $1M in annual revenue that need native invoicing, usage metering, and bespoke contract terms.

use_cases.title: Mid-market revenue operations
use_cases.description: The Core plan adds sales tax integrations and NetSuite/Salesforce connections for companies with $1-10M in annual revenue.

use_cases.title: Scaling enterprise revenue automation
use_cases.description: The Scale plan adds dedicated onboarding, migration services, and custom integrations for companies with $10M+ in annual revenue.
use_cases.audience: Finance teams at scaling B2B companies

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sequence?
paragraphs: Sequence is a revenue operations platform that unifies CPQ (Configure Price Quote), billing, and revenue recognition for B2B companies. It markets itself as an "AI Revenue platform for Next Gen Finance teams," positioning the product around automating revenue collection for custom deals at scale.

The platform is built and operated out of London and New York, and is backed by investors including Andreessen Horowitz and Salesforce Ventures. Its plans support ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR compliance across all tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sequence is for
paragraphs: Sequence is aimed at B2B finance teams managing complex, custom deals rather than simple flat-rate subscriptions. Its Growth plan targets startups under $1M in annual revenue, while Core and Scale serve companies from $1M up through $10M+ in annual revenue that need sales tax handling, ERP/CRM integrations, and dedicated onboarding support.

enrichment.short_description: Subskribe is a Quote-to-Revenue platform combining CPQ, subscription billing, and revenue recognition for SaaS companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Subskribe offers CPQ, subscription billing, and ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition for SaaS companies, now part of DealHub.

features.title: Subskribe CPQ
features.description: Quote generation with multilevel discounting and configurable approval workflows for complex deals.

features.title: Subskribe Billing
features.description: Subscription management, usage-based billing, multi-currency support, and automated dunning.

features.title: Revenue Recognition
features.description: Automated revenue recognition for complex deals, supporting ASC 606 and IFRS 15 compliance.

features.title: DealDesk AI
features.description: AI-powered guided selling with smart summarization, conversational support, and intelligent sales rooms.

features.title: E-signature
features.description: Built-in e-signature for finalizing quotes and contracts without a separate tool.

features.title: Advanced Analytics
features.description: Dashboards for SaaS metrics and reporting across the quote-to-revenue lifecycle.

features.title: Business rules engine (Zeppa)
features.description: Configurable rules engine supporting ramp deals and approval logic.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Salesforce, HubSpot, NetSuite, QuickBooks, Stripe, DocuSign, Avalara, Anrok, and Slack, included at no extra charge.

pros: CPQ and Billing modules can be purchased individually rather than only as a full bundle
All integrations (Salesforce, NetSuite, Stripe, Avalara, and others) are included at no extra charge
Recognized as a G2 High Performer for 7 consecutive quarters in CPQ, Subscription Billing, and Revenue Management
Revenue recognition supports ASC 606 and IFRS 15 compliance
DealDesk AI adds AI-powered guided selling and intelligent sales rooms on top of core CPQ
cons: All plans use custom, quote-based pricing with no published rates
No free trial or free plan is mentioned on the pricing page
Acquired by DealHub in November 2025, which may affect long-term product direction even though Subskribe continues operating under its own name
A sample pricing calculator estimate (around $140,000/year) suggests the platform targets mid-market and larger revenue tiers rather than very small teams

faqs.question: Does Subskribe offer a free trial or free plan?
faqs.answer: No free trial or free plan is listed on the pricing page; pricing is provided through a custom quote.

faqs.question: How much does Subskribe cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is custom and based on product selection, term length, number of seats, and annual billing amount. A sample calculator estimate shows roughly $140,000/year for a mid-market company with $100M+ revenue and 101-200 seats.

faqs.question: Can I buy CPQ and Billing separately?
faqs.answer: Yes, Subskribe CPQ and Subskribe Billing are available as individual modules in addition to the full Quote-to-Revenue platform.

faqs.question: Are integrations included in the price?
faqs.answer: Yes, Subskribe states that all integrations are included free of charge.

faqs.question: Is Subskribe still an independent company?
faqs.answer: Subskribe was acquired by DealHub in November 2025 but continues to operate and deliver its product under the Subskribe name.

faqs.question: What revenue recognition standards does Subskribe support?
faqs.answer: Subskribe's revenue recognition module supports ASC 606 and IFRS 15 compliance.

use_cases.title: Complex B2B quoting
use_cases.description: Subskribe CPQ handles multilevel discounting, approval workflows, and e-signature for complex enterprise deals.
use_cases.audience: Sales operations teams at SaaS companies

use_cases.title: Automated revenue recognition
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Subskribe's revenue recognition module to automate ASC 606 and IFRS 15 compliant reporting for complex deals.

use_cases.title: Unified quote-to-revenue workflow
use_cases.description: The full Quote-to-Revenue platform combines CPQ, billing, and revenue recognition to reduce manual reconciliation from quote to cash.
use_cases.audience: RevOps teams at growing SaaS companies

features: Zero reconciliation from quote to revenue

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Subskribe?
paragraphs: Subskribe is an adaptive Quote-to-Revenue platform that combines CPQ, subscription billing, and revenue recognition for SaaS and enterprise companies. Its DealDesk AI feature adds AI-powered guided selling, smart summarization, and intelligent sales rooms to the quoting process.

The company is headquartered in San Ramon, California, and was acquired by DealHub in November 2025. Subskribe continues to operate and deliver its product under its own name following the acquisition.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Subskribe is for
paragraphs: Subskribe serves fast-growing SaaS companies and enterprises with complex deal structures, including customers such as Chainguard, Zip, Beamery, Vivun, Merge, TrustArc, Torq, and Cognigy. It suits sales operations teams needing multilevel discounting and approval workflows, and finance teams needing ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliant revenue recognition, either as standalone modules or a combined platform.
